ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Ka a noba zaa da di tege;
translated: I am going to the farm;

ref: John's followers took his body and buried it. Then they told Jesus what had happened. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Mark
text: A Gyɔɔn potuuribo da wa de la a o eŋɛ te ũũ, kyɛ gaa te ko Yeezu a duoro. Yeezu Naŋ Ko Noba Turi Anuu Bondirii Ka Ba Di (Mak
translated: The disciples came to Jesus and said, "You are the one who was to come, or are we to expect someone else?"

ref: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: a wa sɔre O ka ka ba na deɛ seene O kparoo nɔzoɔrɔ yoŋ. A baalba na zaa naŋ da seene a O kparoo da nyɛ la sammo.
translated: He was a hunter and used to go with his dog on a journey to search for game. His dog used to bring him the game he hunted and he would then prepare it for eating.

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Ba naŋ da wa kpa O a dagaraa zu baare, ba da de la O kparre ŋmɛ gbaŋ a poŋ taa;
translated: They will take a path that leads to a big tree, they will go there and under the tree they will find a big hole;

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Yelzaa poɔ, yɛ maŋ erɛ korɔ yɛ taaba a seŋ lɛ yɛ naŋ boɔrɔ ka ba erɛ korɔ yɛ; bonso, a begɛ ane a Ŋmen-yelmanneba wuluu tɛgɛ la a lɛ. A Dendɔ-fogloo Yɛlɛ (Luk
translated: Go and tell them, they should not do it again, they are causing confusion, they are making noise; it is not good, I beg of you, my fellow elders, let there be peace, let there be calm. The Dendɔ-fogloo Yɛlɛ (Book)
